title: Ancient DNA Extraction & Spectral Analysis Lab
description: You are a paleogenomics chemist tasked with extracting preserved DNA from a mosquito trapped in amber. Using a broken glass window as a diffraction grating, you must split laser light through the fractured panes to perform spectral analysis on extracted samples, calibrating wavelengths and reagent concentrations while obeying conservation of reagent mass across all vessels.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) implementing an interactive chemistry lab. Scene setup: place the MosquitoInAmber.glb model on a lab bench (left side) as the specimen. Place GlassBrokenWindow.glb upright on a rotating mount (right side) acting as a makeshift diffraction grating. Use PerspectiveCamera at (0,3,7) looking at origin. Add warm ambient light (0.4), a directional light simulating overhead fluorescents, and a colored PointLight representing the laser beam. Add OrbitControls. Interactions (10-14 steps with undo/redo): 1) Click amber specimen to begin dissolution — a slider controls HCl reagent concentration (0-100ml). 2) Pour solvent onto amber using a draggable beaker; amber opacity decreases proportionally. 3) Extract DNA sample into a cuvette (click mosquito after amber dissolves below 20% opacity). 4) Position cuvette in the laser path between a laser emitter and the broken glass. 5) Toggle laser ON — a RaycasterLine shoots from emitter through cuvette to the glass. 6) Rotate the broken glass mount (drag to rotate Y-axis) to change diffraction angle. 7) Observe spectral bands projected onto a virtual screen behind the glass (colored planes whose positions shift with glass rotation). 8) Adjust laser wavelength via RGB sliders, changing the PointLight color and spectral output. 9) Add fluorescent dye reagent to cuvette (second slider) — spectral peaks shift. 10) Record readings by clicking a 'Log' button that stores wavelength+angle+concentration tuples. Conservation law: total reagent volume across all vessels is fixed at 200ml; adding HCl reduces available dye and vice versa, enforced in UI. Multi-input coupling: spectral output depends on laser wavelength × glass angle × dye concentration simultaneously. Implement full undo/redo with Ctrl+Z/Ctrl+Y using a state stack storing all slider values, glass rotation, amber opacity, and log entries. Display a HUD panel (HTML overlay) showing current reagent volumes, angle, wavelength, and logged readings. All models must cast and receive shadows with PCFSoftShadowMap.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  amberOpacity: number,         // current opacity of amber specimen (0-100)
  hclConcentration: number,         // HCl reagent concentration in ml (0-100)
  dnaExtracted: boolean,         // whether DNA sample has been extracted from mosquito
  cuvettePositioned: boolean,         // whether cuvette is placed in laser path
  laserActive: boolean,         // whether laser beam is currently ON
  glassRotationAngle: number,         // Y-axis rotation angle of broken glass mount in degrees (0-360)
  diffractionBands: number,         // count of visible spectral bands on screen (0-7)
  wavelengthCalibrated: boolean,         // whether wavelength calibration is complete
  reagentVolumePoured: number,         // volume of solvent poured onto amber in ml (0-150)
  spectralPeakWavelength: number,         // dominant wavelength detected in nanometers (400-700)
  analysisComplete: boolean,         // whether full spectral analysis has been completed
  laserIntensity: number,         // laser power level percentage (0-100)
}
